title: NDFC POAP / ZTP Bootstrap and Day-0 Onboarding Failures
exclusions: Does not replace physical layer validation (optical power, breakout mapping) in the data hall; does not certify Smart Licensing entitlements alone; does not audit long-run configuration drift after In-Sync (see UC-18.4.2/18.4.10 families); does not observe POAP on switches never routed to NDFC management.
evidence: Saved search uc_18_4_13_ndfc_poap_core, dashboard DC_NDFC_POAP_Failures_18_4_13, weekly CSV of failing serial_number rows archived to network_operations with 13-month retention.
dataSources: `index=cisco_dc` (or `network_operations`) combining **`sourcetype=cisco:ndfc:poap`**, **`sourcetype=cisco:ndfc:discovery`**, **`sourcetype=cisco:ndfc:fabric_status`**, **`sourcetype=cisco:ndfc:audit`**, and **`sourcetype=cisco:ndfc:syslog`**. REST polls target **`GET /appcenter/cisco/ndfc/api/v1/lan-fabric/rest/control/fabrics/{fabric}/switchesBySerialNumber`** and **`GET /appcenter/cisco/ndfc/api/v1/lan-fabric/rest/discovery`** via **Splunk REST API Modular Input** ([**1546**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1546)). Required fields: **`serial_number`**, **`poap_status`**, **`bootstrap_phase`**, **`error_message`**, **`fabric_name`** (alias vendor spellings as needed). Align starter panels with [**7777**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/7777); optional [**4022**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4022), [**4856**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4856), [**1810**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1810) enrich maintenance context.
app: **Splunk REST API Modular Input** ([Splunkbase **1546**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1546)); **Cisco Data Center Networking for Splunk Platform** ([Splunkbase **7777**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/7777)); **Splunk Add-on for Cisco ACI** ([Splunkbase **4022**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4022)); **Splunk Add-on for VMware NSX** ([Splunkbase **4856**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4856)); **Splunk Add-on for VMware** ([Splunkbase **1810**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1810)).
spl:
index=cisco_dc (
    sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:poap" OR sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:discovery" OR sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:fabric_status"
    OR sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:audit" OR sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:syslog"
)
earliest=-24h@m latest=now
| rex field=_raw max_match=0 "(?i)(?<![A-Z0-9])(?P<rex_sn>[A-Z0-9]{11}[A-Z0-9]{0,10})"
| eval st=lower(sourcetype)
| eval sn_norm=upper(trim(coalesce(serial_number,serialNumber,SerialNumber,chassis_serial,chassis_id,chassisSerial,rex_sn,"")))
| eval fabric_key=trim(lower(coalesce(fabric_name,fabricName,fabric,Fabric,lanFabric,nd_fabric,"")))
| eval phase_raw=lower(trim(coalesce(bootstrap_phase,bootstrapPhase,poap_phase,provisioningPhase,stage,Stage,phase,"")))
| eval stat_raw=lower(trim(coalesce(poap_status,poapStatus,status,Status,registrationState,onboardingState,state,health,"")))
| eval err_raw=trim(coalesce(error_message,errorMessage,error_code,errorCode,err_msg,Message,message,detail,reason,""))
| eval from_poap=if(st=="cisco:ndfc:poap",1,0)
| eval from_discovery=if(st=="cisco:ndfc:discovery",1,0)
| eval from_audit=if(st=="cisco:ndfc:audit",1,0)
| eval from_syslog=if(st=="cisco:ndfc:syslog",1,0)
| eval from_status=if(st=="cisco:ndfc:fabric_status",1,0)
| eval dhcp_stuck=if(match(phase_raw,"dhcp|discover|preboot|pobm") AND NOT match(stat_raw,"success|complete|bound|done|ok"),1,0)
| eval image_lane=if(match(phase_raw,"image|kickstart|loader|nxos|software|copy"),1,0)
| eval image_fail=if(image_lane=1 AND match(stat_raw,"fail|error|timeout|abort|corrupt"),1,0)
| eval cert_lane=if(match(phase_raw,"cert|trust|pki|ztp.?token|scm"),1,0)
| eval cert_fail=if(cert_lane=1 AND match(stat_raw,"fail|error|denied|invalid|reject"),1,0)
| eval script_fail=if(match(phase_raw,"script|day0|post.?install|poap") AND match(stat_raw,"fail|error|exit"),1,0)
| eval syslog_story=if(from_syslog=1 AND match(_raw,"(?i)(poap|ztp|bootstrap|power.?on.?auto|poapm)") AND match(_raw,"(?i)(fail|error|timeout|denied|reject|abort)"),1,0)
| eval audit_story=if(from_audit=1 AND match(lower(_raw),"poap|ztp|onboard|bootstrap|serial|day.?0"),1,0)
| eval fabric_pressure=if(from_status=1 AND match(lower(_raw),"unreachable|degraded|out.?of.?sync|major|critical"),1,0)
| eval fail_flag=case(
    match(stat_raw,"fail|error|timeout|abort|stuck|reject"),1,
    dhcp_stuck=1 OR image_fail=1 OR cert_fail=1 OR script_fail=1,1,
    syslog_story=1,1,
    (isnotnull(err_raw) AND err_raw!="" AND match(lower(err_raw),"denied|timeout|fail|unable|cannot|error|abort|reject")),1,
    audit_story=1 AND match(lower(_raw),"fail|error"),1,
    true(),0)
| where fail_flag=1 AND sn_norm!="" AND match(sn_norm,"^[A-Z0-9]{6,}$")
| bin _time span=15m
| stats min(_time) AS first_seen max(_time) AS last_seen dc(_time) AS bucket_spread
    latest(fabric_key) AS fabric_name latest(phase_raw) AS bootstrap_phase latest(stat_raw) AS poap_status latest(err_raw) AS error_message
    sum(from_poap) AS evt_poap sum(from_discovery) AS evt_discovery sum(from_audit) AS evt_audit sum(from_syslog) AS evt_syslog
    sum(from_status) AS evt_status sum(syslog_story) AS evt_syslog_bootstrap sum(fabric_pressure) AS evt_fabric_stress
    BY sn_norm
| eval evidence_score=evt_poap*3+evt_discovery*2+evt_audit+evt_syslog_bootstrap*2+evt_fabric_stress
| eval age_sec=now()-last_seen
| eval freshness=case(age_sec<900,"last_15m",age_sec<3600,"last_hour",age_sec<14400,"last_4h",true(),"stale")
| where evidence_score>=1
| sort -evidence_score -last_seen
| head 400
description: Detects stalled or failed Nexus Dashboard Fabric Controller POAP and ZTP bootstrap attempts by correlating structured LAN Fabric REST streams (per-serial onboarding status, discovery reachability) with fabric health summaries, operator audit trails, and controller or NX-OS syslog narratives when serial_number, poap_status, bootstrap_phase, or error_message indicate DHCP, image, certificate, or day-0 script faults before the switch reaches an In-Sync posture.
value: Keeps brownfield and expansion projects on schedule by surfacing racks that never finish automated onboarding—reducing idle capital, avoiding silent security posture gaps from half-joined devices, and giving automation engineers prioritized evidence tied to templates and policies instead of anecdotal glass-table refresh cycles.
implementation: (1) Land the five cisco:ndfc sourcetypes on index=cisco_dc using 1546 modular inputs for /switchesBySerialNumber and /discovery plus syslog/audit forwarding. (2) Normalize FIELDALIAS maps for serial_number, fabric_name, poap_status, bootstrap_phase, error_message. (3) Save uc_18_4_13_ndfc_poap_core and wire alerting with poap_suppress.csv plus UC-18.4.8 inventory drilldowns.
visualization: (1) Timeline of first_seen→last_seen by serial tinted by bootstrap_phase. (2) Table of fabric_name, poap_status, error_message, evidence_score, evt_poap, evt_discovery, evt_syslog_bootstrap. (3) Single value + sparkline for distinct failing serials (24h). (4) Heatmap bucket_spread by fabric. (5) Alert payload includes first 120 chars of error_message and link to UC-18.4.8 inventory context.
schema: CIM Alerts for severity-style bootstrap faults; Change for audit-backed template and policy edits; Inventory for serial-to-asset joins with UC-18.4.8.
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Alerts only after FIELDALIAS stabilizes vendor keys on cisco:ndfc:* streams; retain raw syslog for regex backfills.

references.title: Cisco NDFC Fabric Controller Configuration Guide - Switches

knownFalsePositives:
**lab_poap_rehearsal** Burn-in labs and staging racks replay automation workflows dozens of times daily. Tag those chassis in the suppress lookup, require a CMDB lab flag, or scope host overrides so rehearsed lease timeouts never wake production on-call.

**controller_api_lag** Cached discovery responses can trail the glass table for several minutes after operators refresh the UI. Compare adjacent poll buckets, demand two consecutive misses, and confirm syslog success lines before opening a bridge call.

**maintenance_dhcp_window** Core DHCP cuts during router refreshes can strand every device hunting fresh leases and flood discovery with identical errors. Align external change calendars (APIC, NSX, vSphere) and auto-suppress when tickets cite relay or scope work.

**dual_forwarder_duplicate** Two heavy forwarders polling the same fabric with different token names double-count identical failure JSON. Deduplicate on serial plus checkpoint hash or enforce one modular input steward per fabric before trusting evidence spikes.

**image_policy_rebaseline** Golden image promotions briefly mark compliant switches as failing until the policy digest finishes. Watch audit rows for template publish verbs and silence alerting until the post-validation job listed on the change clears the serials.

**token_rotation_noise** Scheduled trust-token rotations emit certificate-phase errors until new bundles land on switches. Pair controller audit narratives with PKI change records and extend suppression when the ticket references planned rotation work.

**spurious_rex_capture** Overly permissive regex capture may treat unrelated uppercase strings as serials. Tighten minimum capture length, exclude known OUI patterns, or demand corroborating POAP events before paging syslog-only rows.

**fabric_status_flap** Transient degraded tiles during supervisor switchovers resemble bootstrap storms. Require matching POAP or discovery anomalies or corroborating switch messages before dispatching field services.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Governed logging lane: land telemetry on **cisco_dc** (or **network_operations**) with RBAC that limits who reads operator-class fields while still exposing **fabric_name** and **serial_number** for rack teams.
• **NDFC 12.x+** with LAN Fabric REST reachable at **/appcenter/cisco/ndfc/api/v1/lan-fabric/rest/**; provision a service account whose role allows continuous GET work against the control plane and discovery collections documented for your maintenance release.
• Collection hosts need **HTTPS/443** to the NDFC VIP and proxy rules that explicitly permit **/control/fabrics/{fabric}/switchesBySerialNumber** and **/lan-fabric/rest/discovery** without TLS inspection breakage on automation traffic.
• Install **Splunk REST API Modular Input (Splunkbase 1546)**; bind secrets through **passwords.conf** tied to **restmodinput** stanzas instead of pasting tokens into dashboards or saved-search definitions.
• Add **Cisco Data Center Networking for Splunk Platform (Splunkbase 7777)** as the starter visualization pack, then retarget macros to each **cisco:ndfc** sourcetype enumerated in this UC instead of sample defaults.
• When virtualization calendars matter, optional TAs **4022**, **4856**, and **1810** add APIC/NSX/vSphere maintenance markers so concurrent change noise can be suppressed automatically.
• Finish **UC-18.4.8** (inventory cadence, EoX lookups, hostname joins) before paging on bootstrap-only signals that lack asset context.

Step 1 Configure data collection
• Schedule **GET /appcenter/cisco/ndfc/api/v1/lan-fabric/rest/control/fabrics/{fabric}/switchesBySerialNumber** every **300–900s** per fabric with jitter; flatten JSON so **serial_number**, **poap_status**, **bootstrap_phase**, **error_message**, and **fabric_name** exist as indexed fields after transforms.
• Ingest **GET /appcenter/cisco/ndfc/api/v1/lan-fabric/rest/discovery** as **sourcetype=cisco:ndfc:discovery** to capture pending-device posture and controller-side reachability that clarifies stuck DHCP versus image pulls.
• Normalize automation exports or streaming jobs into **sourcetype=cisco:ndfc:poap** with a written **bootstrap_phase** dictionary shared by NetOps and SecOps so wording stays consistent across labs and production.
• Mirror controller health summaries into **sourcetype=cisco:ndfc:fabric_status** to correlate device-level failures with broader fabric **degraded** or **out-of-sync** states the CLI might not show yet.
• Forward NDFC and NX-OS syslog streams (often **local6**) to **sourcetype=cisco:ndfc:syslog** with correct **TZ** settings; long raw messages backstop detection when REST bodies omit **error_message** text.
• Land configuration and operator JSON in **sourcetype=cisco:ndfc:audit** so bursts of POAP errors line up with **image policy**, boot-variable, or licensing edits made minutes earlier.
• Smoke-test ingestion using **tstats** or **stats** over the last hour to prove each sourcetype is non-zero during business hours when racks are actively turning up hardware.

Step 2 Create the search
Run the SPL below, save **uc_18_4_13_ndfc_poap_core**, then clone an alert with thresholds tuned to your facility.

```spl
index=cisco_dc (
    sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:poap" OR sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:discovery" OR sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:fabric_status"
    OR sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:audit" OR sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:syslog"
)
earliest=-24h@m latest=now
| rex field=_raw max_match=0 "(?i)(?<![A-Z0-9])(?P<rex_sn>[A-Z0-9]{11}[A-Z0-9]{0,10})"
| eval st=lower(sourcetype)
| eval sn_norm=upper(trim(coalesce(serial_number,serialNumber,SerialNumber,chassis_serial,chassis_id,chassisSerial,rex_sn,"")))
| eval fabric_key=trim(lower(coalesce(fabric_name,fabricName,fabric,Fabric,lanFabric,nd_fabric,"")))
| eval phase_raw=lower(trim(coalesce(bootstrap_phase,bootstrapPhase,poap_phase,provisioningPhase,stage,Stage,phase,"")))
| eval stat_raw=lower(trim(coalesce(poap_status,poapStatus,status,Status,registrationState,onboardingState,state,health,"")))
| eval err_raw=trim(coalesce(error_message,errorMessage,error_code,errorCode,err_msg,Message,message,detail,reason,""))
| eval from_poap=if(st=="cisco:ndfc:poap",1,0)
| eval from_discovery=if(st=="cisco:ndfc:discovery",1,0)
| eval from_audit=if(st=="cisco:ndfc:audit",1,0)
| eval from_syslog=if(st=="cisco:ndfc:syslog",1,0)
| eval from_status=if(st=="cisco:ndfc:fabric_status",1,0)
| eval dhcp_stuck=if(match(phase_raw,"dhcp|discover|preboot|pobm") AND NOT match(stat_raw,"success|complete|bound|done|ok"),1,0)
| eval image_lane=if(match(phase_raw,"image|kickstart|loader|nxos|software|copy"),1,0)
| eval image_fail=if(image_lane=1 AND match(stat_raw,"fail|error|timeout|abort|corrupt"),1,0)
| eval cert_lane=if(match(phase_raw,"cert|trust|pki|ztp.?token|scm"),1,0)
| eval cert_fail=if(cert_lane=1 AND match(stat_raw,"fail|error|denied|invalid|reject"),1,0)
| eval script_fail=if(match(phase_raw,"script|day0|post.?install|poap") AND match(stat_raw,"fail|error|exit"),1,0)
| eval syslog_story=if(from_syslog=1 AND match(_raw,"(?i)(poap|ztp|bootstrap|power.?on.?auto|poapm)") AND match(_raw,"(?i)(fail|error|timeout|denied|reject|abort)"),1,0)
| eval audit_story=if(from_audit=1 AND match(lower(_raw),"poap|ztp|onboard|bootstrap|serial|day.?0"),1,0)
| eval fabric_pressure=if(from_status=1 AND match(lower(_raw),"unreachable|degraded|out.?of.?sync|major|critical"),1,0)
| eval fail_flag=case(
    match(stat_raw,"fail|error|timeout|abort|stuck|reject"),1,
    dhcp_stuck=1 OR image_fail=1 OR cert_fail=1 OR script_fail=1,1,
    syslog_story=1,1,
    (isnotnull(err_raw) AND err_raw!="" AND match(lower(err_raw),"denied|timeout|fail|unable|cannot|error|abort|reject")),1,
    audit_story=1 AND match(lower(_raw),"fail|error"),1,
    true(),0)
| where fail_flag=1 AND sn_norm!="" AND match(sn_norm,"^[A-Z0-9]{6,}$")
| bin _time span=15m
| stats min(_time) AS first_seen max(_time) AS last_seen dc(_time) AS bucket_spread
    latest(fabric_key) AS fabric_name latest(phase_raw) AS bootstrap_phase latest(stat_raw) AS poap_status latest(err_raw) AS error_message
    sum(from_poap) AS evt_poap sum(from_discovery) AS evt_discovery sum(from_audit) AS evt_audit sum(from_syslog) AS evt_syslog
    sum(from_status) AS evt_status sum(syslog_story) AS evt_syslog_bootstrap sum(fabric_pressure) AS evt_fabric_stress
    BY sn_norm
| eval evidence_score=evt_poap*3+evt_discovery*2+evt_audit+evt_syslog_bootstrap*2+evt_fabric_stress
| eval age_sec=now()-last_seen
| eval freshness=case(age_sec<900,"last_15m",age_sec<3600,"last_hour",age_sec<14400,"last_4h",true(),"stale")
| where evidence_score>=1
| sort -evidence_score -last_seen
| head 400
```

Understanding this SPL
• **Multi-sourcetype union** matches real triage: REST for structured state, syslog for bootloader narratives, audit for human changes, fabric status for controller stress—none alone captures every POAP/ZTP fault pattern.
• **sn_norm** coalesces serial field variants and uses a tight **rex** backfill only when structured keys were stripped, which keeps false chassis tokens manageable if you adjust the length guardrails per site standard.
• **fail_flag** encodes DHCP/image/cert/script disappointments plus explicit **error_message** substrings so you can extend clauses without rewriting the entire stats stanza.
• **evidence_score** weights **evt_poap** and **evt_discovery** higher than passive status chatter, yet still lets **fabric_pressure** escalate when the controller shouts before the device does.
• **freshness** protects night-shift alerts from ancient rows reindexed during backup replays; tune the **900s** breakpoint if your poll cadence is slower than fifteen minutes.
• **head 400** caps render cost—raise it only after validating drilldown latency on your search head pool.

Step 3 Validate
• Plot **timechart** counts by sourcetype; a flat **cisco:ndfc:poap** line while **cisco:ndfc:discovery** climbs implies the modular input lost credentials, the wrong index received events, or transforms dropped the sourcetype stamp.
• In NDFC's LAN Fabric switch inventory, pick a failing **serial_number** and confirm **poap_status**/**bootstrap_phase** align with Splunk's latest values within a single poll window.
• Compare the Discovery workspace row count to **evt_discovery** trends; divergence almost always maps to timestamp skew, duplicated forwarders, or missed **TIME_PREFIX** settings rather than a real outage.
• Use **fieldsummary** on structured streams until **serial_number**, **fabric_name**, **poap_status**, **bootstrap_phase**, and **error_message** each exceed roughly **85%** prevalence; do not wire paging before that baseline is stable.
• Regression-test a healthy switch: after successful onboarding it must fall out of the failure set when statuses return **success** or **complete** and **error_message** drains to null.

Step 4 Operationalize
• Schedule the saved search every **5–15m** at **priority=medium** so heavy correlation apps on the same search head keep their SLA; add summary indexing only if executives need quarter-long KPIs without raw retention costs.
• Maintain **poap_suppress.csv** with **serial_number**, **suppress_until**, and **reason** for rehearsed burn-ins, golden-template drills, and vendor RMA benches so legitimate lab work never pages prod.
• Limit dashboard access to teams allowed to read detailed **error_message** strings; apply **SEDCMD** or **FIELDALIAS** masking when ticketing connectors would otherwise expose internal management FQDNs.
• Link panels to internal runbooks for DHCP relay, **Option 66/67**, **SCP** reachability, image map completeness, and Smart License pool health—these checkpoints align to the **bootstrap_phase** labels you will sort on.
• Workflow: route syslog-heavy rows to **NOC** first; when **evt_audit** and **fabric_pressure** dominate, escalate toward automation owners who publish templates and policies.

Step 5 Operationalize & Troubleshoot with visualization + alert design
Visualization: (1) Timeline by **sn_norm** from **first_seen** through **last_seen** tinted by **bootstrap_phase**; (2) Table with **fabric_name**, **poap_status**, **error_message**, **evidence_score**, and per-feed counters; (3) Single value plus sparkline for distinct failing serials trailing **24h**; (4) Heatmap of **bucket_spread** vs fabric to catch flickering fabrics; (5) Drill to **UC-18.4.8** lookups for owner, rack, and EoX context.

Alerting: trigger pages when **evidence_score≥6** and **freshness** is **last_15m** or **last_hour**; file tickets only (no voice) for scores **2–5**; throttle repeats per **sn_norm** for **45m** unless **poap_status** changes materially; put the first **120** characters of **error_message** into the notification title for recognition at a glance.

Troubleshooting cheat sheet: expired tokens or proxy auth failures on the **1546** host → silent REST; tail **splunkd.log**. Missing **fabric_name** keys → extend **FIELDALIAS** maps on discovery JSON. High **bucket_spread** with tiny scores → require two consecutive breaches to ignore telemetry flutter. Post-audit spikes → diff the audit payload against golden policy before blaming cabling. Syslog-only hits with dead REST → validate **POAP** config on-switch and physical reachability to filename servers. **rex** hallucinated serials → shorten allowable capture length or add exclude lists for site-specific OUIs.
grandmaExplanation: We watch new switches as they join the fabric through the controller, and we tell you when one stalls during its first-day setup so your team fixes cables or images before empty racks block rollout dates.
hardware: Cisco Nexus platforms undergoing NDFC-managed POAP or ZTP bootstrap
